questions.question: What was the main source of income for the British government in India?
questions.answer: Land revenue was the main source of income for the British government.

questions.question: What was the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: Permanent Settlement was a land revenue system introduced by the British in Bengal.

questions.question: Who introduced the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: Lord Cornwallis introduced the Permanent Settlement in 1793.

questions.question: Who were zamindars?
questions.answer: Zamindars were landowners who collected land revenue from peasants.

questions.question: Who cultivated the land in villages?
questions.answer: Peasants cultivated the land in villages.

questions.question: What is indigo?
questions.answer: Indigo is a plant used to produce blue dye.

questions.question: Why was indigo important for the British?
questions.answer: Indigo was important because it was used to make blue dye for textiles in Europe.

questions.question: Who forced peasants to grow indigo?
questions.answer: European indigo planters forced peasants to grow indigo.

questions.question: What was the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: The Indigo Revolt was a protest by peasants against forced indigo cultivation.

questions.question: When did the Indigo Revolt take place?
questions.answer: The Indigo Revolt took place in 1859–1860.

questions.question: Why did peasants dislike growing indigo?
questions.answer: Peasants disliked growing indigo because it reduced land available for food crops.

questions.question: What did planters give peasants to make them grow indigo?
questions.answer: Planters gave advance loans to peasants.

questions.question: What did peasants do during the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: Peasants refused to grow indigo.

questions.question: Which crop was affected by indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: Food crops were affected by indigo cultivation.

questions.question: What did the British government do after the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: The British government set up the Indigo Commission.

questions.question: Who provided loans to peasants in villages?
questions.answer: Moneylenders provided loans to peasants.

questions.question: Why did peasants borrow money from moneylenders?
questions.answer: Peasants borrowed money to pay taxes and debts.

questions.question: Which region was famous for indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: Bengal was famous for indigo cultivation.

questions.question: What type of crop is indigo?
questions.answer: Indigo is a cash crop.

questions.question: Who controlled indigo plantations?
questions.answer: European planters controlled indigo plantations.

questions.question: What problem did peasants face due to heavy taxes?
questions.answer: Peasants faced debt due to heavy taxes.

questions.question: Why did peasants protest against planters?
questions.answer: Peasants protested because planters forced them to grow indigo.

questions.question: What happened to many peasants because of loans?
questions.answer: Many peasants fell into debt because of loans.

questions.question: Why did the British encourage indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: The British encouraged indigo cultivation to earn profit from exports.

questions.question: What does the Indigo Revolt show about peasants?
questions.answer: It shows that peasants resisted unfair policies.
